title: raw_raise — re-raise exceptions with class/reason/trace
purpose: The existing raise instruction (opcode 108, landed in B-015) re-raises an exception using the class from current_exception() and explicit stacktrace/reason operands. It cannot change the exception class. raw_raise (opcode 161) is the unrestricted form: it reads class, reason, and stacktrace from x(0), x(1), x(2) respectively and raises with all three explicitly specified. This is the instruction the compiler emits for erlang:raise/3 and for re-raising in catch clauses where the original class must be preserved. The instruction is already decoded (Instruction::RawRaise, zero operands) but falls through to UnsupportedOpcode in the dispatch table. Without it, any Erlang or Gleam code that uses erlang:raise/3 or re-raises with an explicit class fails.
task: Implement raw_raise (opcode 161) as a dispatch to raise_exception with class/reason/stacktrace from x registers, validating the class argument. Register erlang:raise/3 as a BIF that delegates to the same logic, adding set_exception_stacktrace to ProcessContext so the BIF error path preserves the stacktrace.

requirements.title: raw_raise opcode handler (opcode 161)
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter encounters Instruction::RawRaise, THE SYSTEM SHALL read x(0) as class, x(1) as reason, and x(2) as stacktrace. THE SYSTEM SHALL validate that class is one of Atom::ERROR, Atom::THROW, or Atom::EXIT_CLASS. WHEN the class is invalid, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ExecError::Badarg. WHEN the class is valid, THE SYSTEM SHALL call raise_exception with the constructed Exception.
acceptance: raw_raise(process) reads: x(0) = class (atom: error, throw, or exit), x(1) = reason (any term), x(2) = stacktrace (any term — typically a list from build_stacktrace, but raw_raise doesn't validate its structure)
Validates class is one of Atom::ERROR, Atom::THROW, or Atom::EXIT_CLASS (atoms added in B-059 R2). If not, returns ExecError::Badarg

Calls raise_exception(process, Exception { class, reason, stacktrace }) — leveraging the existing dispatch logic including handler-kind-aware wrapping (B-059 R3) and raw stacktrace capture (B-061 R1, if landed)
Instruction::RawRaise dispatched in opcodes/mod.rs to the new handler
Test: raw_raise with class=error — exception dispatched to handler, class preserved
Test: raw_raise with class=throw — exception dispatched, throw wrapping in catch handler
Test: raw_raise with class=exit — exception dispatched, exit wrapping in catch handler
Test: raw_raise with invalid class (e.g., atom badclass) — returns Badarg
Test: raw_raise with no handler installed — process exits

requirements.title: erlang:raise/3 BIF registration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL register erlang:raise/3 as a BIF so Erlang code can call it via the standard BIF mechanism. THE SYSTEM SHALL add ProcessContext::set_exception_stacktrace(trace: Term) so the interpreter's BIF error path can construct Exception with the correct stacktrace instead of defaulting to Term::NIL. WHEN the BIF is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL validate the class, set both exception class and stacktrace on the process context, and return Err(reason).
acceptance: erlang:raise/3 registered in the BIF registry with args (Class, Reason, Stacktrace)
NativeFn returns Result<Term, Term> (not Result<Term, ExecError>). The BIF returns Err(reason) to signal an exception.

The BIF must pass both class AND stacktrace through the BIF-to-interpreter boundary. Add ProcessContext::set_exception_stacktrace(trace: Term) so the interpreter's BIF error path can construct Exception { class: take_exception_class(), reason, stacktrace: take_exception_stacktrace() } instead of defaulting stacktrace to Term::NIL.
The BIF implementation: validate class, call process.set_exception_class(class) and process.set_exception_stacktrace(stacktrace), return Err(reason).
The interpreter's BIF error handler reads both fields when constructing the Exception for raise_exception.
Test: call erlang:raise/3 via the BIF mechanism — exception is raised with correct class/reason/stacktrace (not NIL stacktrace)

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify raise_exception — it uses the function as-is from B-059
SHALL NOT implement build_stacktrace (B-061) — raw_raise accepts whatever stacktrace term it's given
verification: cargo test passes with all existing tests plus new raw_raise tests
erlang:raise/3 BIF correctly raises exceptions with preserved class/reason/stacktrace
raw_raise with no handler installed causes process exit
